This stage is the bring-up layer between process startup and the steady-state request loop: it starts or attaches to the app-server, exposes its transports, and gives CLI and TUI callers a usable client/session surface. On the daemon side, app-server-daemon/src/lib.rs is the top-level lifecycle orchestrator, using backend/mod.rs and backend/pid.rs to discover binaries, manage detached PID-file-based server and updater processes, validate stale records, and stop or probe them safely. client.rs is the daemon’s control-socket JSON-RPC/WebSocket probe used for readiness and initialization, while remote_control_client.rs drives enable/disable flows and reconnect-aware status waits; cli/src/doctor/background.rs inspects this machinery without mutating it.

Inside the server, app-server/src/lib.rs boots configuration, telemetry, state, and transport tasks. transport.rs and outgoing_message.rs maintain per-connection routing, capability filtering, callbacks, and broadcasts; initialize_processor.rs gates connections into the initialized state; in_process.rs offers the same protocol without external sockets. app-server-transport supplies stdio, Unix-socket, and TCP websocket listeners plus websocket auth policy. Its remote_control subtree persists desired state, performs enrollment and pairing, tracks multiplexed remote clients, and runs the reconnecting websocket loop. Finally, app-server-client and tui/src/app_server_session.rs wrap these transports into higher-level async APIs for callers.
